title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A practical guide for developers on writing clean, readable, and maintainable code. It covers coding principles, best practices, and real-world examples to improve software craftsmanship.

title: Pragmatic Dev
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: A timeless guide that helps programmers improve their problem-solving skills, coding habits, and career mindset with practical advice and real-world techniques.

title: Algo Basics
author: Thomas H. Cormen
description: A comprehensive introduction to algorithms, covering essential concepts like sorting, searching, data structures, and computational complexity in depth.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: Explains how small daily habits can create significant life changes. It provides actionable strategies to build good habits and eliminate bad ones effectively.

title: Rich Dad
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki
description: Focuses on financial education, teaching the difference between assets and liabilities while encouraging smart investing and wealth-building habits.

title: Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical novel about a shepherd’s journey to follow his dreams, discover his purpose, and listen to his heart.

title: Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story addressing racism and justice in the American South, seen through the eyes of a young girl.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian novel depicting a totalitarian regime where surveillance, censorship, and propaganda control every aspect of life.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Explores the history of humankind, from early evolution to modern societies, examining culture, science, and economics.

title: Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: Introduces a methodology for building startups efficiently through continuous innovation, testing, and customer feedback.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Teaches how to focus deeply in a distracted world, improving productivity and achieving meaningful results.

title: Fast & Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Explains the two systems of thinking—fast and intuitive vs. slow and logical—and how they influence decisions and behavior.
